creator: Alexander Terp
description:
Functions returned from other functions cannot access variables from the enclosing scope. The error is "Undefined variable: <name>". This breaks factory functions, make_adder patterns, make_counter, and passing closures as callbacks.

Closures called *within* the enclosing scope work fine. Only closures that escape (are returned and called later) fail.

**Repro:**
```
fn make_adder(n):
    return fn(x) x + n

add5 = make_adder(5)
print(add5(3))
// Expected: 8
// Actual: error "Undefined variable: n"
```

**Test files:** `bugs/functions/t17c_closure_param.rad`, `t17e_closure_local.rad`, `t87_return_inner_named_closure.rad`, `t88_closure_escape_anon.rad`, `t90_syntax_md_closure_example.rad`, `t111_fn_recursive_closure.rad`, `t125_fn_closure_callback.rad`, `t126_fn_closure_escape_compound.rad`
title: Escaping closures are broken - returned functions lose enclosing scope
